settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Providers, models, API checks, interface language and themes.
settings.languageHint: Interface copy is resolved through localization keys. API parameter values remain provider-facing technical values.
settings.persistApiKey: Save API key in localStorage. Fine for a local personal run; better disabled for deployment.
settings.probeText: Sends tiny test requests to generation/edit endpoints and caches the capability profile locally.
settings.probeRunning: Checking provider… This may take a little time and spend a few credits.
settings.probeEmpty: Capability cache for the current provider is empty. After probing, unsupported parameters will be hidden in the interface.
settings.probeNote: Previously checked providers are restored from localStorage without another probe run.
settings.section.connectionText: Endpoint URLs, model and request timeout.
settings.section.authText: Key, header scheme and storage mode.
settings.info.language: Changes interface copy only. Technical parameter values stay untranslated so API requests remain valid.
settings.info.generationEndpoint: URL for regular generation requests without an edit target image.
settings.info.editEndpoint: URL for edit mode: target image, refs, mask and prompt are sent there.
settings.info.responsesEndpoint: Optional Responses API endpoint. Only needed for providers that use a separate responses route.
settings.info.modelId: Model ID is added to the payload when sending the model is enabled in generation parameters.
settings.info.apiKey: The key is used only for requests from this local interface. Do not publish a build with a saved key.
settings.info.timeout: How many milliseconds to wait for the provider before aborting the request. Heavy generations need more room.
settings.info.authHeader: Authorization HTTP header name. Most OpenAI-compatible APIs use Authorization.
settings.info.authScheme: Prefix before the key. Usually Bearer, but some proxies use another scheme or an empty value.
settings.info.customHeaders: Additional HTTP headers as a JSON object. Useful for organization/project ids or proxy headers.
settings.info.persistApiKey: When enabled, the key survives page reloads in localStorage. Convenient locally, risky on shared devices.
settings.info.probe: The probe sends tiny test payloads and remembers which parameters the provider accepts for generation and editing.
settings.interfaceText: These settings belong to the app shell, not to one specific API.
settings.apiText: One provider stores endpoints, keys and headers; several models can point to that provider.
settings.modelsHint: Model IDs attached to existing providers.
settings.info.providerAdapter: The adapter decides how Image Studio builds the request, sends it through the local proxy and parses the response. Keep this profile for OpenAI-compatible image APIs.
settings.activeModelHint: This model will be used in the bottom composer and generation payload.
settings.noProvidersText: Add at least one provider before attaching models to it.
settings.noModelsText: Add a model and attach it to one of the providers.
settings.info.providerName: Human-readable provider name for lists, generation history and diagnostics.
settings.info.modelName: Human-readable model name in the interface. The API receives the separate technical ID.
settings.info.modelProvider: The provider whose endpoints and authorization will be used by this model.
settings.info.modelNotes: Local note: limitations, cost, or recommended parameters.
settings.themeHint: The theme changes the palette, accent, background effects and typography. Each preview shows the target style, not the currently selected one.
settings.info.theme: The theme applies to the whole shell: gallery, settings, detail page and bottom composer. It does not affect generation payloads.
settings.theme.glass.title: Glass
settings.theme.glass.description: Soft glass, grid background and neutral accent.
settings.theme.midnight.title: Midnight
settings.theme.midnight.description: Neon midnight, drifting particles and Space Grotesk.
settings.theme.ember.title: Ember
settings.theme.ember.description: Warm sparks, amber accent and Manrope.
settings.theme.meadow.title: Meadow
settings.theme.meadow.description: Deep green glow, soft particles and rounded type.
settings.theme.mono.title: Graphite
settings.theme.mono.description: Monospace graphite, scanlines and a pixel snake.
settings.comfy.title: Local ComfyUI resources
settings.comfy.text: Connect a local ComfyUI server, refresh live checkpoints/LoRA and register friendly LoRA presets for the app.
settings.comfy.emptyText: Add a local ComfyUI provider first. It will use the running server on 127.0.0.1:8188 by default.
settings.comfy.info.provider: This provider supplies the base URL and live resource list for checkpoints and LoRA files.
settings.comfy.loraRegistryText: Register local LoRA files under app-friendly names and default strengths.
settings.comfy.noLorasText: Add a LoRA registration after refreshing resources, or enter the file name manually.
settings.comfy.info.loraDisplayName: Human-readable name shown inside Image Studio. The actual ComfyUI file name stays separate.
settings.comfy.info.actualLora: File name from ComfyUI models/loras. Refresh resources to fill this dropdown from the local server.
settings.comfy.info.strength: Default strength used when this LoRA is attached to a ComfyUI request.
settings.comfy.info.checkpointModel: For ComfyUI models this value is selected from live checkpoints instead of typed as an API model id.
settings.comfy.checkpointFallback: Resource list is not loaded yet. You can type the checkpoint manually or refresh resources.
settings.comfy.noCheckpointsFound: ComfyUI is reachable, but no checkpoints were found. Check models/checkpoints or CheckpointLoaderSimple object_info.
settings.comfy.missingRegisteredLora: One or more registered LoRA files are missing from the live ComfyUI list. Update the registration or restore the file in models/loras.
settings.integrations.title: App integrations
settings.integrations.text: Connect Image Studio to external apps through separate server-side adapters. Integration settings stay away from generation providers.
settings.integrations.unsupported: This integration does not have a management panel yet.
settings.integrations.runtimeIdle: Runtime has not been started yet.
settings.integrations.telegram.label: Telegram
settings.telegram.botText: The token is stored only in server encrypted storage. The browser receives only configured state and a short preview.
settings.telegram.httpsHint: Telegram WebAppInfo requires a public HTTPS URL. Local development needs a tunnel or a separate public deployment.
settings.telegram.actionsText: Actions use the saved server-side config. Save the form first when it has changed, so the bot does not run with stale values.
settings.telegram.localDevHint: For local development, use a public HTTPS tunnel or a public staging URL. Telegram clients cannot open local 127.0.0.1 addresses.
settings.telegram.accessText: Polling remains the MVP launch mode. The allowlist limits which users get /start responses from the bot.
settings.telegram.diagnosticsText: See why actions are unavailable and test a bot message or Mini App auth.
settings.telegram.ready.tokenOk: A saved token exists. The UI shows only its preview.
settings.telegram.ready.tokenMissing: Save the Bot API token on the server first.
settings.telegram.ready.urlOk: The URL looks like a public HTTPS address.
settings.telegram.ready.urlMissing: URL is not set yet. Runtime and menu button actions stay unavailable.
settings.telegram.ready.urlInvalid: Telegram WebAppInfo requires an HTTPS URL.
settings.telegram.ready.savedOk: The form matches the saved config.
settings.telegram.ready.savedDirty: There are unsaved changes: save the config first.
settings.telegram.ready.runtimeStopped: Runtime is stopped or has not been started yet.
settings.telegram.reason.busy: Wait for the current action to finish.
settings.telegram.reason.notRunning: The bot is not running right now.
settings.telegram.reason.saveTokenFirst: Save the Bot API token first.
settings.telegram.reason.httpsUrlRequire: Enter a valid HTTPS Mini App URL.
settings.telegram.reason.testChatRequire: Enter a numeric chat id or @channelusername.
settings.telegram.reason.initDataRequire: Paste Telegram Mini App initData to validate it.
settings.telegram.validationMockFailed: Mock token validation failed: Bot API returned 401 Unauthorized.
